title: Red Hat Security Advisory: golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb security update
overview:
An update for golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb security update

An update for golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb-0.9.27-4.el10_1.src.rpm
golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb-debugsource-0.9.27-4.el10_1.x86_64.rpm
ipp-usb-0.9.27-4.el10_1.x86_64.rpm
ipp-usb-debuginfo-0.9.27-4.el10_1.x86_64.rpm
golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb-debugsource-0.9.27-4.el10_1.s390x.rpm
ipp-usb-0.9.27-4.el10_1.s390x.rpm
ipp-usb-debuginfo-0.9.27-4.el10_1.s390x.rpm
golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb-debugsource-0.9.27-4.el10_1.ppc64le.rpm
ipp-usb-0.9.27-4.el10_1.ppc64le.rpm
ipp-usb-debuginfo-0.9.27-4.el10_1.ppc64le.rpm
golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb-debugsource-0.9.27-4.el10_1.aarch64.rpm
ipp-usb-0.9.27-4.el10_1.aarch64.rpm
ipp-usb-debuginfo-0.9.27-4.el10_1.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-61729: Within HostnameError.Error(), when constructing an error string, there is no limit to the number of hosts that will be printed out. Furthermore, the error string is constructed by repeated string concatenation, leading to quadratic runtime. Therefore, a certificate provided by a malicious actor can result in excessive resource consumption.
